titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Willis Photographs of Arizona, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota
Willis (John) Photographs of Arizona, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason and Jennifer Garcia

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 16, 2018 at 21:03

did.unittitle: Photographs of Arizona, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Color photographs of scenes of Pine Ridge, South Dakota, and the surrounding communities. Manuscript indentification on verso. Signed by photographer on verso.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Navajo Nation, and Glen Canyon Dam and Lake Powell.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under six headings: Chinle, Kayenta, Navajo Generating Station, Tuba City, Window Rock, and Dr. Chip Thomas Murals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Navajo Generating Station is a coal-fired powerplant located on the Navajo Nation, near Page, Arizona.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two prints untitled and one of those prints also unsigned by the photographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detail views of weathered United States flags at Navajo Veterans' Cemetery.

did.unittitle: Photographic Murals by Dr. Chip Thomas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of "wheatpaste" photographic poster murals created physician and artist James Edward Thomas, Jr., also known as Dr. Chip Thomas and Jetsonorama.

did.unittitle: Glen Canyon Dam and Lake Powell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 19 inches (33 centimeters x 48.2 centimeters) .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into two subseries: Whiteclay, Nebraska, and Camp Zero Tolerance.

title.part: Whiteclay, Nebraska, Where Over 11,000 Cans of Beer are Sold a Day in a Town with 24 Full Time Residents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Camp Zero Tolerance was a protest camp established in April-June 2013 near the border at Whiteclay, Nebraska, and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation. The camp sought to stop alcohol sales in Whiteclay, Nebraska, to residents of the reservation. In his descriptions, Willis used abbreviations, which this guide spells out, as well as correcting occasional misspellings. Some inscriptions refer to "Camp Zero Justice," which may represent a description error by Willis.

title.part: Sign Referencing Liquor Store Owners in Whiteclay, Nebraska
Tonia Stands, Spokeswoman for Camp Zero Tolerance speaks to the Media
Protesting Alcohol Sales in Whiteclay, Nebraska
Camp Zero Justice Protesters Impose Impromptu Alcohol Check Road Block
Oglala Sioux Tribe Tribal Alcohol Road Block Entering Pine Ridge Reservation from Whiteclay, Nebraska (inspired by Camp Zero Tolerance)
Requesting Implementation of Alcohol Road Block
Bryan Brewer, Oglala Sioux Tribal President, Supporting Camp Zero Tolerance
Strategic Planning with Bryan Brewer Tribal President, Camp Zero Tolerance
Whiteclay, Nebraska, next to Camp Zero Tolerance
Looking Toward Whiteclay, Nebraska, from Pine Ridge Reservation
Border of the Pine Ridge Reservation and Whiteclay, Nebraska
Border of the Pine Ridge Reservation with Whiteclay, Nebraska
Tipi, Border of the Pine Ridge Reservation with Whiteclay, Nebraska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters), of sites in Edgewood, New Mexico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into four subseries: San Haven Tuberculosis Hospital, Turtle Mountain Indian Reservation, Williston, and Unidentified Sites.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of the derelict San Haven State Hospital (established as the North Dakota Tuberculosis Sanitarium in 1909 and vacated in 1989). The Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa Indians of North Dakota purchased the properties from the State of North Dakota in 1992.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints chiefly of a trailer home destroyed by fire.

title.part: RV Park for Oil Boom Housing
Roll of Attendance, Small Town Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 19 inches (33.0 centimeters x 48.3 centimeters) except where noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is organized into nine subseries: Allen, Batesland, Kyle, Manderson, Pine Ridge, Porcupine, Potato Creek and Vicinity, Sharps Corner, and Unidentified Community.

title.part: Alma Richard's Memorial, Allen Community Action Program (CAP) Building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters), of a memorial service for Alma W. Richard (1952-2011).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters), and one inkjet print, 13 inches x 19 inches (33.0 centimeters x 48.3 centimeters).

title.part: American Horse Road, Mobile Home Site

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters), and one inkjet print, 13 inches x 19 inches (33.0 centimeters x 48.3 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Indian Health Service Pine Ridge Hospital, Housing, Oglala Sioux Tribe Fireworks, Sites and Persons.

did.unittitle: Indian Health Service Pine Ridge Hospital

title.part: Federal Emergency Management Agency Trailer Home
Federal Emergency Management Agency Trailer Home (from Katrina Rejects?)
Lakota Skate Boarder, Pine Ridge Skate Park Founding Member

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Sundance Grounds and Sites and Persons.

title.part: Last Year's Prayer Ties, Sundance Grounds
Preparing the 1st Fire from last year's Sundance tree
Tools for Tipi Preparation for Sundance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings: Dullknife Family Home, Morrison Family Home, and Potato Creek Housing.

title.part: Wilbur Morrison with daughter and grandson at Dullknife family Wopila Dinner
Wopila meal at the Dullknife Home
Celinda and Wilbur Morrison's Home, Two Bedroom House with 18 people living in it

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

title.part: Selling Traditional Herbs for Ceremonial Use

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 13 inches x 19 inches (33.0 centimeters x 48.3 centimeters) except where noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is organized into four subseries: Black Hills, Interior, Oglala, and Scenic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kogi, also known as Kagaba, are an indigenous group that lives in the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta in Colombia.

title.part: Kogi Elder Medicine Man Whom Traveled from Columbia to Meet with Lakota Elders
Kogi Elder Medicine Woman Whom Traveled from Columbia to Meet with Lakota Elders
Kogi Medicine Man Whom Traveled from Columbia to Meet with Lakota Elders
Freedom Days, 4th of July Rodeo, Reservation Bordertown
Oglala Lakota College Old Nursing School Dorm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three inkjet prints, 13 inches x 16 inches (33.0 centimeters x 40.6 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, each with two images, 11 inches x 21.5 inches (27.9 centimeters x 54.6 centimeters) except where noted.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VII is organized into four subseries: Nebraska and Camp Zero Tolerance, Pine Ridge Indian Reservation, and South Dakota.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Whiteclay, Nebraska, and Camp Zero Tolerance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Camp Zero Tolerance was a protest camp established in April-June 2013 near the border between Nebraska and the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation to shut down alcohol sales in Whiteclay, Nebraska, to residents of the dry reservation. In his descriptions, Willis used abbreviations, which this guide spells out, as well as correcting occasional misspellings. Some inscriptions refer to "Camp Zero Justice," which may represent a description error by Willis..

title.part: Abandoned Building on the Border of Whiteclay, Nebraska, and Camp Zero Tolerance About 50 Yards Away from Pine Ridge Reservation
Assisting an Inebriated Lakota Woman, Camp Zero Tolerance, After she came from Whiteclay, Nebraska, Reporting Having Been Raped There

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Batesland, Pine Ridge, Porcupine Sundance Grounds, and Potato Creek and Vicinity.

title.part: Santana and Mercedes Little Spotted Horse with Their Boyfriends, 4th of July Powwow (Each Flag Represents a Specific Lakota Veteran)
Warfield Moose, Yuwipi Man and Sundance Leader, the Day the Sundance Arbor was Being Prepared
Dullknife Family and Visitors and the Dullknife Family Home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print with two images, 11 inches x 15 inches (27.9 centimeters x 38 centimeters).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized under two headings: Black Hills and Scenic.

title.part: Tourist's Memory Through Photography, Mount Rushmore National Monument

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print with two images, , 11 inches x 17.5 inches (27.9 centimeters x 44.5 centimeters)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, each with three images, 11 inches x 32 inches (27.9 centimeters x 81.3 centimeters).

title.part: Protester at Camp Zero Tolerance on the Border of Pine Ridge Reservation and Whiteclay, Nebraska
Selling Crafts in Exchange for Alcohol. Whiteclay, Nebraska

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Organized under two headings: Allen and Kyle.

title.part: Preparing the Wopila Meal, Alma Richard's Memorial Service
Trailer House Remains After Wind Damage, American Horse Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints, 24 inches x 31 inches (61 centimeters x 78.7 centimeters) except where noted.

title.part: Navajo Military Graveyard During Spring Winds
Auto Scrap Metal Yard, Three Mile Creek Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 29.25 inches x 24 inches (74 centimeters x 61 centimeters).

title.part: Windswept Trailer House Remains, American Horse Road

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print, 23.5 inches x 59.5 inches (57.7 centimeters x 151.2 centimeters)
